title: landolphia dulcis var. barteri
intro: Landolphia dulcis var. barteri, also known as the African bloodwood or the bitter yam, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which contain alkaloids and glycosides that exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Landolphia dulcis var. barteri include treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use in traditional medicine to boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the roots of the plant are often dried, crushed, and made into decoctions or infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
